title: Nathas I

'''Nathas I''' was a [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] and [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]] rocky [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Questal sector/Legends|Questal sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Silicon/Legends|silicon]]-based [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifters]] known as [[Protean]]s. Although seemingly primitive, they noted key historical events by carving stone pillars and placing them in prominent locations on their home planet. The Proteans were eventually discovered and briefly studied by [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Description==
Nathas I was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> located in the [[Nathas system/Legends|Nathas system]], a part of the [[Questal sector/Legends|Questal sector]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="EAOC" /> A terrestrial world, it occupied the first [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]].<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

Nathas I was a [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] and [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]] planet with rocks on its surface, albeit without any valuable [[Ore/Legends|ore]] deposits. There were no [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] [[Species/Legends|creatures]] on Nathas I, and the fossil record did not suggest the presence of predators at any point in the world's past.<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

==History==
When Nathas I was first [[Scout/Legends|surveyed]],<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] discovered the planet's [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] species and tentatively named them "[[Protean]]s."<ref name="Journal12">{{JournalCite|12|Shape-shifters}}</ref> The scientists proceeded to briefly study the species, but the planet was soon passed over due to the lack of any strategic or economic importance.<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> One Protean, [[Lonchant]], had found its way offworld onto the planet [[Questal/Legends|Questal]] by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], where it served Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Bandor (Moff)|Bandor]] as a [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] and killer.<ref name="Questal">''[[The Game Chambers of Questal]]''</ref>

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Protean}}
[[File:Protean-SWcom.jpg|thumb|right|170px|The Proteans were native to Nathas I.]]
The only sizable life form on Nathas I were the Proteans, a species of amorphous, [[Silicon/Legends|silicon]]-based [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifters]]. Due to the airless nature of their homeworld, the Proteans communicated with each other via chemical and tactile signals exchanged when in direct contact. In addition, they had evolved a skin lined with tympanic nerves<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> that were most likely used to sense vibrations along the ground of Nathas I.<ref name="Journal12" /> A notably unusual feature of the Proteans, given their airless natural environment, was their ability to funnel air through their bodies and modulate it, creating tonal variations in what was a form of wordless "[[Music/Legends|songs]]."<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

The seemingly-primitive Proteans themselves were not aware of the purpose for which they had evolved their shape-changing ability, and the species did not maintain [[Writing/Legends|written records]]. However, they did commemorate key historical events<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> by carving unusual<ref name="Journal12" /> stone pillars that were placed in prominent locations on Nathas I.<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

==Behind the scenes==
The homeworld of the Proteans was first mentioned by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] in his [[February]] [[1997]] article "[[Shape-shifters]]," included in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 12|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 12]].<ref name="Journal12" /> It was first identified as Nathas I in ''[[Alien Encounters]]'', a [[1998]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Nathas system, and therefore Nathas I, in grid square J-12.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
